subject: People, people, people
body:
| | |

| |

|
---
|
---
| **_People, People, People_** This week, **Governor Eric Holcomb** delivered his second State of the State address. Like the President's State of the Union (which **President Trump** will give later this month), the State of the State is the Governor's opportunity to lay out his big-picture priorities for legislators and Hoosiers across the state.

And wow, did he _deliver_ on laying out those priorities! At the heart of his speech were three priorities -- which were _people, people, and people_.

As our state's chief executive, Governor Holcomb goes to work daily with the sole focus of helping every Hoosier -- from the oldest to the very youngest -- live life to the fullest.

**Governor Holcomb, Lt. Governor Crouch** and our Republican team in the Indiana General Assembly -- led by House **Speaker Brian Bosma** and**Senate President Pro Tem David Long** \-- are working together on very specific goals the Governor laid out this week. These goals attack the biggest challenges our state faces today, ranging from skilling up Hoosiers to placing an opioid treatment center within one hour's drive of every Hoosier.

And just like Governor Holcomb is focused on people, here at the Indiana Republican Party, so are we. __**More than 500 Hoosiers**__ are currently attending Congress of Counties this weekend, our grassroots training conference for local Republican party leaders, candidates, future candidates and volunteers.

Whether it's the work led by Governor Holcomb and our Republican team at the Statehouse, or our candidates and grassroots volunteers stepping up to elect more Republicans, as Hoosiers, we're working hard to help build a better life for all Hoosiers \-- something we can only achieve together.

-Kyle Hupfer
---
| |
---
|
---
| **Make Your Voice Heard at Congress of Counties!**
---
| More than 500 Hoosiers are attending Congress of Counties this weekend, our SOLD OUT grassroots political training conference that began today and runs through Saturday. Right now, these Hoosiers are learning critical campaign skills that will help us all secure more Republican victories throughout the state in 2018 and beyond!

Our big event is already in action, but there's **_still time_** to register for our**_U.S. Senate Straw Poll_ **on Saturday! If you haven't registered yet, [click here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f99457ab50&e=1acd9bb3d6) to purchase a standing room only ticket to vote in our straw poll, and make your voice heard in this first election-year appearance from our candidates!
---
|
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---
| It was a big week for Governor Holcomb! During his State of the State address, he laid out a bold, transformative plan that will work to make life better for all Hoosiers, including goals to:
* **Achieve specific, measurable workforce benchmarks** – such as:
* Helping at least 25,000 of the 700,000+ Hoosiers with some college but no degree gain a post-secondary credential
* Helping at least 30,000 of the 475,000 Hoosiers without a high school diploma gain the education they need to get a better, higher-wage jobs
* **Cultivate Indiana’s strong and diversified economy** by:
* Requiring every K-12 school to offer computer science courses
* Clarifying Indiana’s tax laws regarding Software as a Service (SaaS) companies
* **Maintain and build Indiana infrastructure** by:
* Opening Section 5 of I-69
* Building capacity to resurface 10,000 lane miles of pavement and repair or replace 1,300 bridges over the next five years
* **Attack the drug epidemic** by:
* Making sure everyone in the state is less than one hour’s drive from an opioid treatment center
* **Deliver great government service** by:
* Conducting a thorough assessment of the Indiana Department of Child Services and providing two progress reports to the public during the legislative session
* Setting a goal for Indiana to become the best state in the Midwest for infant mortality rates by 2024

Did you miss Governor Holcomb's speech on Tuesday? No problem! [You can watch it now here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c889fc26bb&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

**Congresswoman Jackie Walorski**[expressed that all human life is precious](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=597c5844e2&e=1acd9bb3d6)[ & should be protected](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=cfd06acfe1&e=1acd9bb3d6)

name: Team Kaine
subject: SURVEY: We want to hear from you about offshore drilling >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
A lot's been happening with offshore drilling in the last week. Here's the short of it:

First, Trump announced that he would be expanding offshore drilling anywhere and everywhere -- a move that would negatively affect countless communities and threaten Virginia's coastal tourism and naval operations in Hampton Roads. (It's also completely unnecessary, but that's a whole other email.)

Then, after the Governor of Florida spoke up, Secretary of the Interior Ryan Zinke announced that there would be no drilling off the coast of Florida. As Zinke said, "local voice matters" -- and he had heard loud and clear that offshore drilling would be bad for Florida.

**Sure, that seems right. But what about other coastal states -- like, say, Virginia? Both Terry McAuliffe and Ralph Northam have spoken up, too, and we have yet to hear of any exemptions for the Commonwealth. So it begs the question: Why exactly is Florida the exception here?
**
Could it possibly be that the Mar-a-Lago resort would be negatively impacted? (Or the "Winter White House" as some call it...) Could it be because the Governor of Florida is a Republican, while Virginia's leaders are Democrats? Could it be that Florida is a key state that the Republican administration is eager to have on its side? **No matter which way you cut it, as a recent Washington Post editorial put it, excluding Florida "sure looks partisan."**

**So here's what we need from you: If Secretary Zinke is determined to listen to local voices, we must speak up.[Will you fill out this quick, one-minute survey to share your opinion on the recent offshore drilling news?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=ygNehUPT%2FAIDTNdZcjDMdqNnvOpQbY0o)**

Thanks -- we really appreciate your input.

-Team Kaine

subject: Tell Oregon's governor: Stop state wolf hunts
body:
(https://environmental-action.webaction.org/p/dia/action4/common/public/?action_KEY=23916&tag=Source:ExpPaid&tag=ad:EAC_EAC_CNS_1117_Dems_rd2_MRSS&utm_source=Dems&utm_medium=CPA&utm_campaign=EAC4-SCNS:WILDLIFE:PREDATOROTHR-1117&utm_content=EM9:00L:0HH-AGE)

It's up to us to stop Oregon's state-sponsored wolf hunts.

Activist,

Just nine wolves, including 3 pups, make up the Harl Butte pack, ranging across woods and mountains in eastern Oregon. But 4 of them have been tracked, hunted and killed by the state of Oregon in the last year, and some ranchers have asked them to kill the rest.1

Tell Oregon Governor Kate Brown: Stop the state wolf hunts. (https://environmental-action.webaction.org/p/dia/action4/common/public/?action_KEY=23916&tag=Source:ExpPaid&tag=ad:EAC_EAC_CNS_1117_Dems_rd2_MRSS&utm_source=Dems&utm_medium=CPA&utm_campaign=EAC4-SCNS:WILDLIFE:PREDATOROTHR-1117&utm_content=EM9:00L:0HH-AGE)

Four wolves killed is four too many -- every wolf is precious, and there are barely a hundred of them in the entire state of Oregon. None of them threatened humans, and they can be ordered killed for no more than preying on a handful of unattended cattle, often on public land.2 Of course no rancher wants to lose livestock, but the state already reimburses them for lost cattle.3

(https://environmental-action.webaction.org/p/dia/action4/common/public/?action_KEY=23916&tag=Source:ExpPaid&tag=ad:EAC_EAC_CNS_1117_Dems_rd2_MRSS&utm_source=Dems&utm_medium=CPA&utm_campaign=EAC4-SCNS:WILDLIFE:PREDATOROTHR-1117&utm_content=EM9:00L:0HH-AGE)

This isn't just happening in Oregon -- Washington, Idaho and other states also actively hunt wolves for similar reasons. They hunt them from aircraft and bait them with sweet-smelling poisons like cyanide traps.4 5 But right now we're focusing on Oregon as the most likely place where we can stop this senseless killing. Please tell Gov. Brown: This is just plain wrong. Stop the state wolf hunts. (https://environmental-action.webaction.org/p/dia/action4/common/public/?action_KEY=23916&tag=Source:ExpPaid&tag=ad:EAC_EAC_CNS_1117_Dems_rd2_MRSS&utm_source=Dems&utm_medium=CPA&utm_campaign=EAC4-SCNS:WILDLIFE:PREDATOROTHR-1117&utm_content=EM9:00L:0HH-AGE)

>From standing up for Cascade-Siskiyou National Monument to urging action on climate change, Gov. Brown has a strong track record on the environment. She inherited this wolf-killing policy from her predecessor, and it's not too late for her to stop it -- but she needs to hear from you that stopping wolf hunts is the right thing to do. Tell Gov. Brown: No more state-funded killing of wolves. (https://environmental-action.webaction.org/p/dia/action4/common/public/?action_KEY=23916&tag=Source:ExpPaid&tag=ad:EAC_EAC_CNS_1117_Dems_rd2_MRSS&utm_source=Dems&utm_medium=CPA&utm_campaign=EAC4-SCNS:WILDLIFE:PREDATOROTHR-1117&utm_content=EM9:00L:0HH-AGE)

In the 1900s, we nearly hunted wolves to extinction, and they were entirely wiped out in Oregon. They returned for the first time just 18 years ago, and there are still barely more than 100 wolves in the entire state. It's up to us to keep them from being wiped out again. Please act today. (https://environmental-action.webaction.org/p/dia/action4/common/public/?action_KEY=23916&tag=Source:ExpPaid&tag=ad:EAC_EAC_CNS_1117_Dems_rd2_MRSS&utm_source=Dems&utm_medium=CPA&utm_campaign=EAC4-SCNS:WILDLIFE:PREDATOROTHR-1117&utm_content=EM9:00L:0HH-AGE)

Thank you,

Sally, Marcia, Pat and the Environmental Action team

1. "Harl Butte Pack (http://www.dfw.state.or.us/news/2017/08_Aug/80317.asp) ," Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife, Accessed Sept. 20, 2017.
2. Eric Mortensen, "ODFW approves killing two Harl Butte pack wolves (http://www.capitalpress.com/Oregon/20170803/odfw-approves-killing-two-harl-butte-pack-wolves) ," Capital Press, August 4, 2017.
3. Amaroq Weiss and Wally Sykes, "It's time to stop killing Oregon's wolves (Guest opinion) (http://www.oregonlive.com/opinion/index.ssf/2017/09/its_time_to_stop_killing_orego.html) ," Oregon Live, September 13, 2017.
4. Madeline Farber, "Why Washington Decided to Kill Off Once-Endangered Wolves From Helicopters (http://time.com/4472562/washington-wolves-profanity-peak-pack/) ," Time, August 30, 2016.
5. Cassandra Profita, "Conservation Groups Sue To Protect Wildlife From 'Cyanide Bombs' (https://www.opb.org/news/article/conservation-groups-sue-to-protect-wildlife-from-cyanide-bombs/) ," Oregon Public Broadcasting, April 4, 2017.

name: The White House
subject: Resolute Reads
body:
1600 Daily REAL NEWS PRESIDENT TRUMP DOESN'T WANT YOU TO MISS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b463acc322958b9e8d80a00489d957e1ac3c7e2f687301baea86b644ce79684ef65d267e6627b3e0a8263eee762152b80a02c2d57402a8d4
Border Patrol union: Morale was 'low as it could get' under Obama, but Trump's support has 'helped'

- Washington Examiner

This week on Capitol Hill, Jon Anfinson, President of Local 2366 of the National Border Patrol Council, testified to lawmakers that under President Obama, “morale was probably about as low as it could get” among border patrol agents. “I think part of the improvement has been that we have an administration that clearly supports what we do.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b463acc322958b9e50dede892ad88e3dc36c5538268e8336c077bd17c1c9b33e10e11a6901e509aa2c01ba26ff70e78118e0078a08aadf68

Toyota, Mazda announce $1.6 billion plant for Huntsville, Alabama

- Reuters

This week, Toyota Motor Corp and Mazda Motor Corp announced that they will build a $1.6 billion joint assembly plant in Alabama. This plant will employ up to 4,000 workers and will produce 300,000 vehicles a year. The President tweeted about the news, congratulating Alabama and saying “companies are coming back to the U.S. in a very big way.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b463acc322958b9ec1359b15b0e0c06e63d93072b965d92e949e1eda049b8c169b11bc2eefee3b979cb07c38dbcb76692406bd389ca90002
Holiday retail sales increased 5.5 percent in 2017, exceeding NRF forecast and showing strongest gain since Great Recession

- National Retail Federation

Yesterday, the National Retail Federation released new data showing that holiday retail sales the final two months of 2017 exceeded forecasts, showing the strongest gain since the Great Recession. The Federation credited this increase to growing wages, stronger employment, and higher consumer confidence.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b463acc322958b9ec1c1e5bde31235c4476028305ed1a5a316768dc1c9e4fad839e9363c580f17658f366939edd91a3eee3310c08cb8d3d7

Tax cuts hit home

- The Washington Times

An editorial in The Washington Times slams those who claimed that President Donald J. Trump’s tax reform efforts would end in doom and gloom, citing myriad ways that tax cuts have already hit home for American workers, including raises, bonuses, and improved family policies. “’Are you better off than you were two years ago?’ For many [workers], the answer is obvious.”

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b463acc322958b9efd8581a73f2a4255795957848ee5a9aa55a80c8a46b8b888928c984e8f723e584f0e7a8f1dfb267e48590417af23dde8
Americans' optimism about job market hit record high in 2017

- Gallup News

The latest polling shows that Americans’ optimism about the U.S. job market hit record highs in 2017—“a sharp increase” from 2016. “Coinciding with rising optimism, the U.S. unemployment rate fell from an average 4.9% in 2016 to 4.4% in 2017, the lowest rate since 2000,” Gallup News reports.

-------------------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b463acc322958b9eb7019b98d4730c00e2601288cc68447a0607246e2fddc70b946787e7a580feb976e715f8ed1b4f15691eddbb1dec9c09
Trump looks to slash government red tape on overseas sale of U.S. made weapons

- The Washington Times

As part of the President’s “Buy American” initiatives, the Trump Administration is looking to “streamline the weapons export process and speed up the pace of deals” for overseas sales of weapons. American arms manufacturers believe these changes will “even the playing field with their less regulated foreign competitors and result in billions of dollars in new deals.”

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b463acc322958b9ead68d80414d3ed6157d5b8f73893b7a3409e3536624bab9f74840dac9863f1634811d50d177999a5ce67b12fb7348b19
Privacy Policy |

Contact the White House |

subject: [SURVEY] Tell us what issues you care about
body:
Take our quick survey to share which issues are most important to you.
---
**Share which issues are most important to you:

** **[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/issues-survey?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+TAKE+THE+SURVEY&utm_campaign=em_20180113_ke_nd&source=em_20180113_ke_nd")**
The Democratic Party is your party, Derek -- and that's why we need to hear from you right now.

[**Take our brief survey to let us know what issues you care about most so we can organize together and win.**](https://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/issues-survey?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Take+our+brief+survey+to+let+us+know+wha&utm_campaign=em_20180113_ke_nd&source=em_20180113_ke_nd")

2017 was a challenging year, with the GOP forcing their reckless agenda onto the American people. But for every challenge we faced, we rolled up our sleeves -- and as a result, we celebrated some historic victories.

As we step into 2018, we know there's still much more to do and we can't take our foot off the gas if we want to keep bringing the fight to Republicans. That's why we need your input.

**Take a few minutes to complete our survey to let us know what issues are important to you:**

**[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/issues-survey?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+TAKE+THE+SURVEY&utm_campaign=em_20180113_ke_nd&source=em_20180113_ke_nd")**
Thanks for all you do,

Keith

Deputy Chair
Democratic National Committee

subject: And the straw poll winner is...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://us11.campaign-archive.com/?e=1acd9bb3d6&u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9bc90c0455)
---
| |
---
| | **Congressman Luke Messer Wins Congress of Counties Straw Poll**
---
|
---
| Congratulations to Congressman Luke Messer, the winner of today’s U.S. Senate Straw Poll at our SOLD OUT Congress of Counties political training conference!

Indiana Republican Party Chairman Kyle Hupfer said it best:

**_“Today’s straw poll shows that Hoosier Republicans are fired up about electing a U.S. Senator who will vote to cut taxes, reform health care and grow jobs in Indiana. Hoosiers are tired of hearing one Joe Donnelly in Indiana, and a vastly different Donnelly in Washington, where he consistently votes with Chuck Schumer and his fellow Democrats on the issues that matter most. We’re united as Republicans in our mission to send one of these candidates to Washington and make Joe Donnelly a one-term senator.” _**

Our six Republican candidates made their first joint election-year appearance today at Congress of Counties, which brought more than 500 grassroots Hoosier Republicans -- including candidates, volunteers and Republican Party leaders -- to Indianapolis over the two-day conference to build important skills to deliver more Republican victories up and down the ballot in November.

As part of the straw poll, Congressman Messer received 147 total votes. Congressman Todd Rokita came in second, receiving 82 total votes, followed by former State Rep. Mike Braun with 36 votes, Mark Hurt with 29 votes, Andrew Takami with 20 votes and Andrew Horning with 12 votes.

It's clear that Hoosier Republicans like you are fired up about our 2018 elections, and the work we'll be doing together over the coming months to elect one of these six candidates as our next U.S. Senator and to fire Democrat Joe Donnelly!
---

name: Kelly
subject: when I signed on
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Way back in 2011, when I joined Joe's first campaign for Senate, the national press didn't give us a chance. At the time, they saw Joe as a long-shot candidate running against a well-funded, popular, |
---

| | Peter,

Way back in 2011, when I joined Joe's first campaign for Senate, the national press didn't give us a chance. At the time, they saw Joe as a long-shot candidate running against a well-funded, popular, establishment incumbent.
| [](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2252/7377f838/32c0e17c/51fa0d8b/2133321588/VEsH/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/share-your-story")
---
Back then, a lot of folks were counting us out. We were reading that the Republican wave that swept through Indiana in 2010 would continue with the help of outside groups flooding the state with negative ads. Those groups flooded the state with dark money attacks almost as soon as Joe's extremist opponent won his primary.

**But we knew better. On the ground in Indiana, we were starting to feel something _special_ happening.** At event after event, voter after voter would come up to us and tell Joe he was different -- that he was speaking for them.

They respected the fact that Joe stayed true to who he was and ran on his record, including his vote for the Affordable Care Act. **They saw that by sending him to the Senate, they 'd be electing a fighter who would work for them.** And in November of 2012, that's exactly what they did.

That's enough from me. I want to know why it is that you support Joe -- and why this race matters to you, whether you've been with us since the beginning or joined the team this year. If you don't mind, will you share your story?
**[**SHARE YOUR STORY >>**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2252/7377f838/32c0e17c/51fa0d8b/2133321588/VEsE/ "https://donnelly.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/share-your-story")**
---
Joe's race is already heating up. With election forecasters and pundits calling Joe "the nation's most vulnerable senator" and ranking the race "leans Republican," we need to see the same level of grassroots support that we saw in 2012 -- and then some -- to win in November.

**Thank you for your support, whether you 've been with us since 2011 or hopped on board more recently. Let's bring it home for Joe again.**

Thank you,

Kelly

name: Jane Kleeb, Our Revolution
subject: Water is Life
body:
Republican and Democratic governors alike have called
#
Friends,
President Donald Trump and his fossil fuel-infiltrated Department of the Interior just announced their plan to massively expand offshore oil drilling, and nobody seems happy about it. Republican and Democratic governors alike have called for their coastlines to be exempted from oil drilling, but President Trump is already picking favorites. He has exempted his resort property, Mar-a-Lago, and the rest of Florida from offshore drilling while placing millions of people's homes and livelihoods at risk.
[**Donate $5 right now so that we can fight back against the political cronyism and pay-to-play politics that Republicans have let stand for democracy. With your help, we can fully fund more than 500 local groups across the country to organize and take our government back from fossil fuel special interests.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad88/bed43eb/355c65a0/75fea347/860913345/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/act-on-climate?refcode=em180113-full&noskip=true")
It is painfully obvious that this administration is happy to take money from the fossil fuel industry, but when their home states are the ones threatened by offshore oil drilling, they trot out the old mantra "Not in my backyard." We must apply that logic to the United States as a whole, not just states governed by their political allies.
The question is not if a spill will occur, but when. Exploiting finite resources for profit hurts all of us. Our Revolution is firmly in support of attaining 100% renewable energy and immediately ceasing all new fossil fuel projects, including offshore drilling. Renewable energy is cleaner, cheaper, and can support millions of new jobs.
[**Make a $5 contribution to Our Revolution and help us bring an end to new fossil fuel projects across the country. More than 500 local Our Revolution groups are organizing across the country to ensure our government represents all people, not special interests. Chip in to boost their efforts today.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad88/bed43eb/355c65a0/75fea347/860913345/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/act-on-climate?refcode=em180113-full&noskip=true")
The American people see the hypocrisy and double standards of this administration and their fossil fuel industry cronies. It's up to us to organize, fight back, and win. Thank you for supporting Our Revolution and protecting our environment today.
In solidarity,
Jane Kleeb
Board Member
Our Revolution
